task_completion.description: Navigate to the 'Add New User' page in the WordPress/WooCommerce admin panel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Firefox browser shows the WordPress admin interface with the 'Add New User' screen open.

task_completion.description: Enter the correct Username and Email.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Username' field contains 'sarahjohnson' and the 'Email' field contains 'sarah.johnson@example.com'.

task_completion.description: Enter the correct First Name and Last Name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'First Name' field contains 'Sarah' and the 'Last Name' field contains 'Johnson'.
The 'Role' dropdown menu is set to 'Customer'.
A success message (e.g., 'New user created.') is visible, or the 'Users' list page is shown displaying the newly created user 'sarahjohnson' with the role 'Customer'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Firefox web browser to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Firefox browser window is visible and being interacted with, rather than a script or API tool.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the genuine WordPress/WooCommerce admin interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The standard WordPress admin sidebar (dark grey usually on the left) and top bar are visible.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands or database clients to bypass the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows, command-line interfaces, or database management tools (like phpMyAdmin) are used to insert the user directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The VLM should verify the following exact details for the new user account: Username: 'sarahjohnson', Email: 'sarah.johnson@example.com', First Name: 'Sarah', Last Name: 'Johnson', Role: 'Customer' (or 'customer'). Any password can be used.
